rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER SARKANY ET AL VS. CHRISTIE WEST ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Finding Of Contempt Against Defendant And Debtor Christie West; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant And Debtor Christie West
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 11, 2025 line 1. PLAINTIFF JENNIFER SARKANY, RAMSEY ABOUREMELEH, SANDRA FIERRO, NINA ROBINS NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR FINDING OF CONTEMPT AGAINST DEFENDANT AND DEBTOR CHRISTIE WEST; REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANT AND DEBTOR CHRISTIE WEST is DENIED without prejudice. Request for contempt is denied. Moving party fails to cite any authority for the proposition that service of the Contempt request by electronic service on Defendant's counsel is proper. Moving party also fails to provide any evidence or authority to support their contention that defendant has the ability to comply with the Court's discovery order. Motion for sanctions is denied. Moving party fails to cite any authority supporting its request for sanctions (cost of brining this motion for contempt and sanctions) as the time of expenses contemplated by CCP 2023.030(a) ("reasonable expenses, including attorney's fees, incurred by anyone as a result of that conduct.") =(501/HLJ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER SARKANY ET AL VS. CHRISTIE BARRETT WEST ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Requiring Defendants To Deposit Rents Into Escrow
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 11, 2025 line 2. PLAINTIFF JENNIFER SARKANY, RAMSEY ABOUREMELEH, SANDRA FIERRO, NINA ROBIN NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER REQUIRING DEFENDANTS TO DEPOSIT RENTS INTO ESCROW is DENIED without prejudice. Moving party fails to support its requested relief ("requiring defendants to deposit rents into escrow") with authority. None of the cases or statutes cited in the Points and Authorities provide for such relief. Plaintiff may file a motion seeking appropriate relief (e.g. under CC 3439.07) or supporting the requested relief with proper authorities that are factually and legally on point. =(501/HLJ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
